title: The benchmarks for protein-protein, protein-DNA, and protein-RNA docking
author: Banciul, Rita
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The benchmarks for protein-protein, protein-DNA, and protein-RNA docking   
 This dataset contains structures of three docking benchmarks, as well as docking tables. This dataset together with the FTDMP framework (https://github.com/kliment-olechnovic/ftdmp) can be used for docking and scoring complexes, as well as evaluating new scoring functions. 
 The PDB IDs and structure chain IDs were taken from protein-protein [1], protein-RNA [2-5], and protein-DNA [6] benchmarks. The structures of complexes from all the benchmarks were downloaded directly from the Protein Data Bank (PDB) [7]. All the structures, including complex substituting bound, and unbound monomers have been renumbered according to the FASTA file of the complex. The B-DNA for unbound DNA-protein docking was modeled with ChimeraX [8]. 
 More information and an example of usage can be found in the README.txt file.

 This dataset contains structures of three docking benchmarks, as well as docking tables. This dataset together with the FTDMP framework (https://github.com/kliment-olechnovic/ftdmp) can be used for docking and scoring complexes, as well as evaluating new scoring functions.

The structures of complexes from all the benchmarks were downloaded directly from the Protein Data Bank (PDB) [7]. All the structures, including complex substituting bound, and unbound monomers have been renumbered according to the FASTA file of the complex. The B-DNA for unbound DNA-protein docking was modeled with ChimeraX [8].
